revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakanoyama Kofun | native_name =中の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Nakanoyama Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Naka-no-yama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|19.9|N|139|28|41.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakanoyama Kofun'''|中の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[Sue ware]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Tumulus in [[Naju]], [[South Jeolla Province]], [[South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/nakano.html Gyoda city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/奥の山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gyōda]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Sakitama Kofun Cluster]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakanoyama Kofun | native_name =中の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Nakanoyama Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Naka-no-yama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|19.9|N|139|28|41.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakanoyama Kofun'''|中の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[Sue ware]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Tumulus in [[Naju]], [[South Jeolla Province]], [[South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakanoyama Kofun | native_name =中の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Nakanoyama Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Naka-no-yama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|19.9|N|139|28|41.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakanoyama Kofun'''|中の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[Sue ware]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Tumulus in [[Naju]], [[South Jeolla Province]], [[South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakanoyama Kofun | native_name =中の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Nakanoyama Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Naka-no-yama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|19.9|N|139|28|41.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakanoyama Kofun'''|中の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[Sue ware]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Tumulus in [[Naju]], [[South Jeolla Province]], [[South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline}} *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/nakano.html Gyoda city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/奥の山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Gyōda]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakanoyama Kofun | native_name =中の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Nakanoyama Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Naka-no-yama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|19.9|N|139|28|41.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakanoyama Kofun'''|中の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[Sue ware]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Tumulus in [[Naju]], [[South Jeolla Province]], [[South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[Sue ware]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Tumulus in [[Naju]], [[South Jeolla Province]], [[South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[Baekje]].

The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[Sue ware]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Tumulus in [[Naju]], [[South Jeolla Province]], [[South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakanoyama Kofun | native_name =中の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Nakanoyama Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Naka-no-yama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|19.9|N|139|28|41.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakanoyama Kofun'''|中の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[Sue ware]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in [[Naju]], [[South Jeolla Province]], [[South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakanoyama Kofun | native_name =中の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Nakanoyama Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Naka-no-yama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|19.9|N|139|28|41.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakanoyama Kofun'''|中の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[Sue ware]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in [[Naju]], [[South Jeolla Province]], [[South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakanoyama Kofun | native_name =中の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Nakanoyama Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Naka-no-yama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|19.9|N|139|28|41.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakanoyama Kofun'''|中の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[Sue pottery]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in [[Naju]], [[South Jeolla Province]], [[South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakanoyama Kofun | native_name =中の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Nakanoyama Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Naka-no-yama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|19.9|N|139|28|41.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakanoyama Kofun'''|中の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[Sue pottery]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in [[Naju]], [[South Jeolla Province]], [[South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakanoyama Kofun | native_name =中の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Nakanoyama Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Naka-no-yama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|19.9|N|139|28|41.7|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakanoyama Kofun'''|中の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[Sue pottery]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in [[Naju]], [[South Jeolla Province]], [[South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[Sue pottery]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in [[Naju]], [[South Jeolla Province]], [[South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[:en:Yorii, Saitama|Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Hita, Oita|Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in [[:en:Naju|Naju]], [[:en:South Jeolla Province|South Jeolla Province]], [[:en:South Korea|South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[en:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[ja:中の山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11362767]] Wikidata name: Nakanoyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakanoyama Kofun | native_name =中の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Gyoda Nakanoyama Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Naka-no-yama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.122194444444|N|139.47825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|WD=Q860659}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakanoyama Kofun'''|中の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[:en:Yorii, Saitama|Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Hita, Oita|Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in [[:en:Naju|Naju]], [[:en:South Jeolla Province|South Jeolla Province]], [[:en:South Korea|South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[:en:Yorii, Saitama|Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Hita, Oita|Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in [[:en:Naju|Naju]], [[:en:South Jeolla Province|South Jeolla Province]], [[:en:South Korea|South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[:en:Yorii, Saitama|Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Hita, Oita|Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in [[:en:Naju|Naju]], [[:en:South Jeolla Province|South Jeolla Province]], [[:en:South Korea|South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high

Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[:en:Yorii, Saitama|Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Hita, Oita|Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in [[:en:Naju|Naju]], [[:en:South Jeolla Province|South Jeolla Province]], [[:en:South Korea|South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high

align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakanoyama Kofun'''|中の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in [[:en:Yorii, Saitama|Yorii, Saitama]] about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Hita, Oita|Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in [[:en:Naju|Naju]], [[:en:South Jeolla Province|South Jeolla Province]], [[:en:South Korea|South Korea]] in what was once the kingdom of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high

Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in {{ill|Yorii, Saitama|en|Yorii, Saitama|WD=Q961604|lt=Yorii, Saitama}} about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Hita, Oita|Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in {{ill|Naju|en|Naju|WD=Q42083|lt=Naju}}, {{ill|South Jeolla Province|en|South Jeolla Province|WD=Q41161|lt=South Jeolla Province}}, {{ill|South Korea|en|South Korea|WD=Q884|lt=South Korea}} in what was once the kingdom of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]].

align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakanoyama Kofun'''|中の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in {{ill|Yorii, Saitama|en|Yorii, Saitama|WD=Q961604|lt=Yorii, Saitama}} about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Hita, Oita|Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in {{ill|Naju|en|Naju|WD=Q42083|lt=Naju}}, {{ill|South Jeolla Province|en|South Jeolla Province|WD=Q41161|lt=South Jeolla Province}}, {{ill|South Korea|en|South Korea|WD=Q884|lt=South Korea}} in what was once the kingdom of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[en:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[ja:中の山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[en:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[ja:中の山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[en:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[ja:中の山古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakanoyama Kofun | native_name =中の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Gyoda Nakanoyama Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Naka-no-yama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.122194444444|N|139.47825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|WD=Q860659|1=Gyōda}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580|1=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakanoyama Kofun'''|中の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in {{ill|Yorii, Saitama|en|Yorii, Saitama|WD=Q961604|lt=Yorii, Saitama|1=Yorii, Saitama}} about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Hita, Oita|Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in {{ill|Naju|en|Naju|WD=Q42083|lt=Naju|1=Naju}}, {{ill|South Jeolla Province|en|South Jeolla Province|WD=Q41161|lt=South Jeolla Province|1=South Jeolla Province}}, {{ill|South Korea|en|South Korea|WD=Q884|lt=South Korea|1=South Korea}} in what was once the kingdom of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|WD=Q6573282|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)}}

Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in {{ill|Yorii, Saitama|en|Yorii, Saitama|WD=Q961604|lt=Yorii, Saitama|1=Yorii, Saitama}} about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Hita, Oita|Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in {{ill|Naju|en|Naju|WD=Q42083|lt=Naju|1=Naju}}, {{ill|South Jeolla Province|en|South Jeolla Province|WD=Q41161|lt=South Jeolla Province|1=South Jeolla Province}}, {{ill|South Korea|en|South Korea|WD=Q884|lt=South Korea|1=South Korea}} in what was once the kingdom of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[en:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[ja:中の山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[en:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[ja:中の山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[en:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[ja:中の山古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakanoyama Kofun | native_name =中の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Gyoda Nakanoyama Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Naka-no-yama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.122194444444|N|139.47825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|WD=Q860659|1=Gyōda}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580|1=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakanoyama Kofun'''|中の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in {{ill|Yorii, Saitama|en|Yorii, Saitama|WD=Q961604|lt=Yorii, Saitama|1=Yorii, Saitama}} about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Hita, Oita|Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in {{ill|Naju|en|Naju|WD=Q42083|lt=Naju|1=Naju}}, {{ill|South Jeolla Province|en|South Jeolla Province|WD=Q41161|lt=South Jeolla Province|1=South Jeolla Province}}, {{ill|South Korea|en|South Korea|WD=Q884|lt=South Korea|1=South Korea}} in what was once the kingdom of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|WD=Q6573282|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)}}

A grey {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in {{ill|Yorii, Saitama|en|Yorii, Saitama|qid=Q961604|lt=Yorii, Saitama|1=Yorii, Saitama}} about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Hita, Oita|Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in {{ill|Naju|en|Naju|qid=Q42083|lt=Naju|1=Naju}}, {{ill|South Jeolla Province|en|South Jeolla Province|qid=Q41161|lt=South Jeolla Province|1=South Jeolla Province}}, {{ill|South Korea|en|South Korea|qid=Q884|lt=South Korea|1=South Korea}} in what was once the kingdom of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|qid=Q6573282|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[en:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[ja:中の山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[en:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[ja:中の山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakanoyama Kofun]][[ja:中の山古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] | name = Nakanoyama Kofun | native_name =中の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Gyoda Nakanoyama Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Naka-no-yama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakanoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.122194444444|N|139.47825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|qid=Q860659|1=Gyōda}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|qid=Q2213580|1=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakanoyama Kofun'''|中の山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakanoyama Kofun has a total length of 79 meters, and is the third smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Nakanoyama Kofun had a double moat which is presumed to be rectangular as with the other tumuli in this cluster, but this has yet to be confirmed. [[Archaeological excavation]] were performed in 1981, 1987, 1990, and 2010, indicating that it was made from the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, making it the last of the keyhole-shaped tumuli to have been built in this cluster.
A grey {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} jar was found in an excavation of the moat, instead of the usual ''haniwa''. This jar has a trumpet-shaped mouth and a hole in the bottom. Although analysis showed that it was made at a kiln in {{ill|Yorii, Saitama|en|Yorii, Saitama|qid=Q961604|lt=Yorii, Saitama|1=Yorii, Saitama}} about 30 kilometers away, no other examples have been found in the Kantō region, although similar jars have been found at the Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Hita, Oita|Hita, Oita]] and at the Fushiiwasato No. 2 Kofun in {{ill|Naju|en|Naju|qid=Q42083|lt=Naju|1=Naju}}, {{ill|South Jeolla Province|en|South Jeolla Province|qid=Q41161|lt=South Jeolla Province|1=South Jeolla Province}}, {{ill|South Korea|en|South Korea|qid=Q884|lt=South Korea|1=South Korea}} in what was once the kingdom of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]].
;Overall length: 79 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 42 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 44 meters wide x 5.4 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|qid=Q6573282|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)}}
